question_text: A student pulls a wooden block across a rough floor with a force of 50 N. The friction force acting on the block is 20 N. Calculate the net force acting on the block.
explanation: The net force is found by subtracting the friction force from the applied force. Net force = Applied force − Friction force = 50 N − 20 N = 30 N. This net force causes the block to accelerate in the direction it is being pulled.
